+---
+assignees:
+- claude-code
+position_column: todo
+position_ordinal: 9f80
+project: spatial-nav
+title: Spatial-nav kernel returns the focused moniker on "no motion" — eliminate Option<Moniker> from nav/drill APIs
+---
+## What
+
+Replace the kernel's `Option<Moniker>` return shape on `BeamNavStrategy::next`, `SpatialRegistry::drill_in`, and `SpatialRegistry::drill_out` with a non-optional `Moniker`. When motion is not possible, the kernel **returns the focused entry's own moniker** rather than `None`. The React side never sees a silent "kernel had no answer" state; every nav / drill dispatch produces an observable focus state.
+
+This is an architectural change. The current `Option` shape lets every "no motion possible" case look identical at the call site to "kernel lost the registry reference" — torn-state bugs, race conditions during virtualizer remounts, and stale rects all silently produce `None` and the user sees nothing happen. The fix flips the contract so silence is impossible.
+
+## Why None is wrong everywhere
+
+Per the enumeration we just walked through, the kernel today returns None across twelve distinct paths spanning three APIs. Some are deliberate semantics (override wall, layer-root, leaf has no children) and some are torn state (unknown key, orphan parent reference). The call site cannot tell them apart, and the React side falls through silently in either case. Concrete consequences observed in the project:
+
+- **Click-to-focus failures inside scrolled columns** (`01KQ9XBAG5P9W3JREQYNGAYM8Y`) — partly because `focus(unknownKey)` silently fails when the kernel hasn't yet registered the freshly-minted SpatialKey.
+- **Up/Down nav going to "no focus"** (`01KQ5PEHWTEVTKPS2JHSZTXNBE`, `01KQ9XBAG5P9W3JREQYNGAYM8Y`) — partly because stale rects produce no in-beam candidate and the cascade returns None at layer-root.
+- **Inspector field nav requiring users to click to recover** — the unified cascade *almost* always falls through to drill-out (returning the parent zone's moniker), but the layer-root edge case still returns None. Users at the layer root pressing arrow keys silently see nothing.
+
+The user-facing principle: **a key press always produces a focus state, either a new one or the existing one, never absence.** That makes every test trivially observable (focus state before vs after) and turns torn-state into a visible bug instead of a silent one.
+
+## Scope of the contract change
+
+Three APIs change shape:
+
+### `BeamNavStrategy::next` (cardinal + edge)
+
+Today: `fn next(...) -> Option<Moniker>`.
+After: `fn next(...) -> Moniker`.
+
+- Override wall (`overrides: { right: None }`) → return focused.moniker (focus stays put visibly).
+- Layer-root with no peers and no parent to escalate to → return focused.moniker.
+- Cross-layer barrier (parent zone is in a different `LayerKey`) → return focused.moniker.
+- Torn parent reference (parent zone unregistered) → return focused.moniker.
+- Edge commands (`First/Last/RowStart/RowEnd`) — already include focused in the candidate pool, so this is a no-op for them at the implementation level; only the type changes.
+
+The one path where we currently can't return focused.moniker is **unknown focused key** — the kernel has nothing to return because it doesn't know what `focused` resolves to. Two acceptable shapes:
+
+- **Option A**: keep `next`'s input as a `SpatialKey` and return `Result<Moniker, NavError>` with a `NavError::UnknownKey(SpatialKey)` variant. Torn-state surfaces as a typed error the React side can log / recover from explicitly, never as silent None.
+- **Option B**: require the React side to also pass the focused `Moniker` so the kernel can echo it back when the key is unknown. Simpler signature, slightly more redundant data.
+
+**Decide via the failing tests.** Both shapes produce the same observable outcome (no silent failure) — pick whichever the call sites lean toward.
+
+### `SpatialRegistry::drill_in`
+
+Today: `fn drill_in(&self, key: SpatialKey) -> Option<Moniker>`.
+After: `fn drill_in(&self, key: SpatialKey) -> Moniker`.
+
+- Zone with children → return first/remembered child's moniker (unchanged behavior).
+- Zone with no children → return zone's own moniker (was None).
+- Leaf focused → return leaf's own moniker (was None).
+- Unknown key → same shape as `next`'s unknown-key case (`Result` or pass-through Moniker).
+
+**This breaks the existing fall-through pattern in `nav.drillIn` and `field.edit`** that depends on None to mean "fall through to edit / no-op". The new contract: callers compare the returned moniker to the focused moniker. If equal, no descent happened; the caller decides what else to do (edit mode, no-op, beep). If different, focus moves to the returned moniker.
+
+So `field.edit` becomes:
+
+```ts
+const result = await actions.drillIn(fieldZoneKey);
+if (result !== focusedMoniker) {
+  setFocus(result);
+  return;
+}
+// drillIn was a no-op (no children) — fall through to edit mode.
+onEdit?.();
+```
+
+The semantic that "drill_in on a leaf with no editor still does nothing visible" stays — the React side detects equality and chooses no-op. But now the kernel never returns None.
+
+### `SpatialRegistry::drill_out`
+
+Today: `fn drill_out(&self, key: SpatialKey) -> Option<Moniker>`.
+After: `fn drill_out(&self, key: SpatialKey) -> Moniker`.
+
+- Scope with `parent_zone` → return parent's moniker (unchanged).
+- Layer-root scope (no parent) → return focused's own moniker (was None).
+- Torn parent reference → return focused's own moniker (was None).
+- Unknown key → same shape decision as `next` / `drill_in`.
+
+**This breaks the existing fall-through in `nav.drillOut` that dispatches `app.dismiss` on a None result** (closing the topmost layer). Updated semantics: `nav.drillOut` compares result to focused. If equal, no zone-level drill happened; dispatch `app.dismiss` to handle the layer-pop case (close inspector, palette, etc.). The user-observable behavior is unchanged; only the internal signal changes.
+
+## Approach
+
+### 1. Kernel — flip the return types
+
+`swissarmyhammer-focus/src/registry.rs` and `swissarmyhammer-focus/src/navigate.rs`:
+
+- `BeamNavStrategy::next` → `Moniker` (or `Result<Moniker, NavError>`).
+- `cardinal_cascade`, `edge_command`, `edge_command_from_candidates` → `Moniker`.
+- `parent_zone_in_same_layer` stays `Option<&FocusZone>` — internal helper, not part of the public contract.
+- `check_override` → translate the wall case (`Some(None)`) to `Some(focused.moniker.clone())` at the cascade level so the change doesn't leak into the override resolver.
+- `SpatialRegistry::drill_in`, `SpatialRegistry::drill_out` → `Moniker`.
+- `SpatialState` wrappers in `swissarmyhammer-focus/src/state.rs` → match the new shape.
+
+Pick `Result<Moniker, NavError>` over `Moniker` for the unknown-key case if (and only if) the React side benefits from typed error handling. Otherwise have the React side pass the focused `Moniker` explicitly so the kernel can echo it back.
+
+### 2. Tauri commands — propagate the new shape
+
+`kanban-app/src/spatial.rs` (or wherever the Tauri bindings live) — `spatial_navigate`, `spatial_drill_in`, `spatial_drill_out` change return type. Wire shape between Rust and JS becomes `Moniker` (a string brand) instead of `Moniker | null`.
+
+### 3. React side — adjust callers
+
+`kanban-app/ui/src/lib/spatial-focus-context.tsx`:
+
+- `SpatialFocusActions.navigate(key, direction)` returns `Promise<Moniker>` (not `Promise<Moniker | null>`).
+- `drillIn(key)` and `drillOut(key)` likewise.
+
+`kanban-app/ui/src/components/app-shell.tsx`:
+
+- `buildNavCommands` (NAV_COMMAND_SPEC closures): no logic change — just `await actions.navigate(key, dir)` then `setFocus(result)`. No null check. If the result equals the previous focused moniker, `setFocus` is idempotent (the entity-focus store is identity-stable on the same moniker), so a "no motion" press is genuinely a no-op without any conditional.
+- `nav.drillIn` closure: same — `setFocus(result)`. The "leaf with no editor" case is now a no-op via setFocus(self), not via skipping the dispatch.
+- `nav.drillOut` closure: compare result to current focused moniker. If equal, dispatch `app.dismiss`; otherwise `setFocus(result)`. The compare needs the previous focused moniker — read it from the focused-key's entry, or have the closure capture it before the await.
+
+`kanban-app/ui/src/components/fields/field.tsx` (where `01KQ9X3A9NMRYK50GWP4S4ZMJ4` introduces `field.edit`, possibly already merged or not yet):
+
+- Update `field.edit`'s execute closure per the snippet above: drillIn → if result moniker !== focused moniker, setFocus; else onEdit.
+- This makes `01KQ9X3A9NMRYK50GWP4S4ZMJ4` and `01KQ9ZJHRXCY8Z5YT6RF4SG6EK`'s drill-into-pills logic both fall out naturally — the same compare-to-focused check handles both "drilled into pill" (different moniker) and "no children, fall through to edit" (same moniker).
+
+### 4. Tests — update every existing assertion
+
+Every test that asserts `assert_eq!(target, None)` or `expect(result).toBeNull()` becomes either an equality check against the focused moniker, or — where it makes sense — a removed assertion (the test was checking a contract that no longer exists). New tests pin the focused-moniker fallback at every previously-None path.
+
+The full list of tests to update:
+
+- `swissarmyhammer-focus/tests/drill.rs` — every `assert_eq!(target, None)` becomes `assert_eq!(target, focused.moniker)`.
+- `swissarmyhammer-focus/tests/navigate.rs` — same for cardinal nav at layer-root cases.
+- `swissarmyhammer-focus/tests/walls.rs` (or equivalent) — wall override now returns focused.moniker.
+- `kanban-app/ui/src/components/*.test.tsx` — any spatial-nav test asserting `null`/`undefined` from a kernel call.
+
+### 5. Document the contract
+
+Top-of-module docstring on `swissarmyhammer-focus/src/lib.rs` (or `navigate.rs`) explaining the contract: nav/drill APIs always return a `Moniker`. "No motion possible" is communicated by returning the focused entry's own moniker. Torn-state surfaces as `Result::Err` (Option A) or echoes the input moniker (Option B). The React side detects "stay put" by comparing the returned moniker to the previous focused moniker.
+
+## Acceptance Criteria
+
+All asserted by automated tests below — no manual smoke step.
+
+### Kernel contract
+
+- [ ] `BeamNavStrategy::next` returns `Moniker` (or `Result<Moniker, NavError>` per the chosen shape). The type signature does not include `Option<Moniker>` anywhere in the public nav/drill API surface.
+- [ ] `SpatialRegistry::drill_in` returns `Moniker` (or `Result<...>`). Same.
+- [ ] `SpatialRegistry::drill_out` returns `Moniker` (or `Result<...>`). Same.
+
+### Cardinal nav
+
+- [ ] `next(focused, dir)` at the layer-root edge (no peer, no parent) returns `focused.moniker`.
+- [ ] `next(focused, dir)` with an override wall (`overrides: { dir: None }`) returns `focused.moniker`.
+- [ ] `next(focused, dir)` with a torn parent reference returns `focused.moniker`.
+- [ ] `next(focused, dir)` in the normal cascade case (peer / cross-zone / drill-out) returns the new focus moniker as today — no change to in-motion behavior.
+
+### Drill-in
+
+- [ ] `drill_in(zone)` with children returns the first/remembered child's moniker (unchanged).
+- [ ] `drill_in(zone)` with no children returns `zone.moniker` (was None).
+- [ ] `drill_in(leaf)` returns `leaf.moniker` (was None).
+
+### Drill-out
+
+- [ ] `drill_out(scope)` with a parent zone returns the parent's moniker (unchanged).
+- [ ] `drill_out(layer_root_scope)` returns `scope.moniker` (was None).
+- [ ] `drill_out(scope)` with a torn parent reference returns `scope.moniker` (was None).
+
+### React-side semantics preserved
+
+- [ ] `nav.drillOut` dispatches `app.dismiss` when the kernel returns the focused moniker (the new "no zone to drill out to" signal). Inspector / palette closing behavior unchanged.
+- [ ] `field.edit` enters edit mode when `drillIn` returns the focused moniker. Drills into pills when it returns a different moniker. (Replaces the current `null`-based fall-through.)
+- [ ] Pressing a cardinal direction key at the layer root produces no observable focus change but `useFocusedScope()` continues to report the same moniker — no `null` / `undefined` blip in any test that watches `useFocusedScope` over time.
+
+### No silent dropouts
+
+- [ ] No production code path through the spatial-nav stack produces a `null` / `None` focus state observable to the user. Asserted by a corpus-level test sweep: every `assert_eq!(_, None)` / `expect(_).toBeNull()` in spatial-nav tests is updated to check for either the focused moniker (the new "no motion" signal) or the new typed error variant.
+
+## Tests
+
+All tests are automated. No manual verification.
+
+### Rust kernel — `swissarmyhammer-focus/tests/no_silent_none.rs` (new file)
+
+Pins the new contract at every previously-None path. Reuses fixtures from `swissarmyhammer-focus/tests/fixtures/` introduced by `01KQ7STZN3G5N2WB3FF4PM4DKX`.
+
+- [ ] `nav_at_layer_root_returns_focused_moniker_not_none` — register a leaf at the layer root with no peers; `next(leaf, Direction::Up)` returns `leaf.moniker`.
+- [ ] `nav_with_wall_override_returns_focused_moniker` — register a leaf with `overrides: { Right: None }`; `next(leaf, Direction::Right)` returns `leaf.moniker`.
+- [ ] `nav_with_torn_parent_returns_focused_moniker` — register a scope whose `parent_zone` references a key that is not registered; `next(scope, dir)` returns `scope.moniker`.
+- [ ] `drill_in_zone_with_no_children_returns_zone_moniker` — register a zone with no child scopes; `drill_in(zone)` returns `zone.moniker`.
+- [ ] `drill_in_leaf_returns_leaf_moniker` — register a leaf; `drill_in(leaf)` returns `leaf.moniker`.
+- [ ] `drill_out_layer_root_returns_focused_moniker` — register a scope with `parent_zone = None`; `drill_out(scope)` returns `scope.moniker`.
+- [ ] `drill_out_torn_parent_returns_focused_moniker` — `drill_out` with parent_zone pointing at an unregistered key; returns `scope.moniker`.
+- [ ] `unknown_key_returns_typed_error_or_input_moniker` — pin whichever shape Option A vs B is chosen.
+
+Test command: `cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-focus --test no_silent_none` — all eight pass.
+
+### Rust kernel — update existing tests
+
+- [ ] `swissarmyhammer-focus/tests/drill.rs` — replace every `assert_eq!(target, None)` with the new focused-moniker contract. The pre-existing intent of each test is preserved; only the assertion shape changes.
+- [ ] `swissarmyhammer-focus/tests/navigate.rs` — same for cardinal nav.
+- [ ] Any other test in `swissarmyhammer-focus/tests/` asserting `None` from a public kernel API — update.
+
+Test command: `cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-focus` — full crate test suite passes after the migration.
+
+### Frontend — `kanban-app/ui/src/components/spatial-nav.no-silent-dropout.browser.test.tsx` (new file)
+
+Pins the React-side contract.
+
+- [ ] `arrow_at_layer_root_keeps_focus_on_same_moniker` — focus a leaf at the layer root, fire ArrowUp, await one tick, assert `useFocusedScope()` returns the same moniker as before. (No null blip.)
+- [ ] `drill_out_at_layer_root_dispatches_dismiss_with_focus_unchanged` — focus a panel zone (inspector layer root), fire Escape, assert `app.dismiss` was dispatched AND focus stayed on the panel zone moniker until the inspector layer pops (at which point focus moves to the parent layer's last_focused via the existing pop chain).
+- [ ] `field_edit_enters_edit_mode_when_drillIn_returns_focused_moniker` — focus an editable scalar field, fire Enter, assert `editing` flipped to `true` and DOM focus is on the editor input. (Confirms the new compare-to-focused fall-through replaces the old null fall-through.)
+- [ ] `field_edit_drills_into_pill_when_drillIn_returns_different_moniker` — focus a pill field, fire Enter, assert focus moves to the first pill. (Same as `01KQ9ZJHRXCY8Z5YT6RF4SG6EK`'s pill drill-in case but driven by the new contract.)
+
+Test command: `bun run test:browser spatial-nav.no-silent-dropout.browser.test.tsx` — all four pass.
+
+### Frontend — update existing tests
+
+- [ ] Any spatial-nav test that asserts `null` / `undefined` from `actions.navigate`, `actions.drillIn`, `actions.drillOut` — update to compare against the focused moniker.
+
+Test command: `bun run test:browser` — full UI test suite passes after the migration.
+
+## Workflow
+
+- Use `/tdd` — write the kernel-level `no_silent_none.rs` tests first, watch them fail (current contract returns None), flip the kernel return types, watch them pass. Then update the React side to match the new contract; update existing tests; finally pin the browser-level no-silent-dropout assertions.
+- Single ticket — one architectural concern, applied uniformly to three APIs, with a single new principle ("no motion → return focused moniker"). Do not split into one card per API.
+- This change reorganizes how the React side detects "no motion" but does NOT change user-observable behavior (with one exception: `nav.drillOut` at layer-root → `app.dismiss` dispatch chain stays correct). Every existing user-flow continues to work; the silent-dropout torn-state cases get fixed as a side effect.
+- Coordinate with `01KQ9X3A9NMRYK50GWP4S4ZMJ4` and `01KQ9ZJHRXCY8Z5YT6RF4SG6EK`: their `field.edit` execute closures change shape under the new contract. If those tickets land before this one, this ticket updates their closures. If this ticket lands first, those tickets author their closures against the new contract directly.

changes.value:
## What

Replace the kernel's `Option<Moniker>` return shape on `BeamNavStrategy::next`, `SpatialRegistry::drill_in`, and `SpatialRegistry::drill_out` with a non-optional `Moniker`. When motion is not possible, the kernel **returns the focused entry's own moniker** rather than `None`. The React side never sees a silent "kernel had no answer" state; every nav / drill dispatch produces an observable focus state.

This is an architectural change. The current `Option` shape lets every "no motion possible" case look identical at the call site to "kernel lost the registry reference" — torn-state bugs, race conditions during virtualizer remounts, and stale rects all silently produce `None` and the user sees nothing happen. The fix flips the contract so silence is impossible.

## Why None is wrong everywhere

Per the enumeration we just walked through, the kernel today returns None across twelve distinct paths spanning three APIs. Some are deliberate semantics (override wall, layer-root, leaf has no children) and some are torn state (unknown key, orphan parent reference). The call site cannot tell them apart, and the React side falls through silently in either case. Concrete consequences observed in the project:

- **Click-to-focus failures inside scrolled columns** (`01KQ9XBAG5P9W3JREQYNGAYM8Y`) — partly because `focus(unknownKey)` silently fails when the kernel hasn't yet registered the freshly-minted SpatialKey.
- **Up/Down nav going to "no focus"** (`01KQ5PEHWTEVTKPS2JHSZTXNBE`, `01KQ9XBAG5P9W3JREQYNGAYM8Y`) — partly because stale rects produce no in-beam candidate and the cascade returns None at layer-root.
- **Inspector field nav requiring users to click to recover** — the unified cascade *almost* always falls through to drill-out (returning the parent zone's moniker), but the layer-root edge case still returns None. Users at the layer root pressing arrow keys silently see nothing.

The user-facing principle: **a key press always produces a focus state, either a new one or the existing one, never absence.** That makes every test trivially observable (focus state before vs after) and turns torn-state into a visible bug instead of a silent one.

## Scope of the contract change

Three APIs change shape:

### `BeamNavStrategy::next` (cardinal + edge)

Today: `fn next(...) -> Option<Moniker>`.
After: `fn next(...) -> Moniker`.

- Override wall (`overrides: { right: None }`) → return focused.moniker (focus stays put visibly).
- Layer-root with no peers and no parent to escalate to → return focused.moniker.
- Cross-layer barrier (parent zone is in a different `LayerKey`) → return focused.moniker.
- Torn parent reference (parent zone unregistered) → return focused.moniker.
- Edge commands (`First/Last/RowStart/RowEnd`) — already include focused in the candidate pool, so this is a no-op for them at the implementation level; only the type changes.

The one path where we currently can't return focused.moniker is **unknown focused key** — the kernel has nothing to return because it doesn't know what `focused` resolves to. Two acceptable shapes:

- **Option A**: keep `next`'s input as a `SpatialKey` and return `Result<Moniker, NavError>` with a `NavError::UnknownKey(SpatialKey)` variant. Torn-state surfaces as a typed error the React side can log / recover from explicitly, never as silent None.
- **Option B**: require the React side to also pass the focused `Moniker` so the kernel can echo it back when the key is unknown. Simpler signature, slightly more redundant data.

**Decide via the failing tests.** Both shapes produce the same observable outcome (no silent failure) — pick whichever the call sites lean toward.

### `SpatialRegistry::drill_in`

Today: `fn drill_in(&self, key: SpatialKey) -> Option<Moniker>`.
After: `fn drill_in(&self, key: SpatialKey) -> Moniker`.

- Zone with children → return first/remembered child's moniker (unchanged behavior).
- Zone with no children → return zone's own moniker (was None).
- Leaf focused → return leaf's own moniker (was None).
- Unknown key → same shape as `next`'s unknown-key case (`Result` or pass-through Moniker).

**This breaks the existing fall-through pattern in `nav.drillIn` and `field.edit`** that depends on None to mean "fall through to edit / no-op". The new contract: callers compare the returned moniker to the focused moniker. If equal, no descent happened; the caller decides what else to do (edit mode, no-op, beep). If different, focus moves to the returned moniker.

So `field.edit` becomes:

```ts
const result = await actions.drillIn(fieldZoneKey);
if (result !== focusedMoniker) {
  setFocus(result);
  return;
}
// drillIn was a no-op (no children) — fall through to edit mode.
onEdit?.();
```

The semantic that "drill_in on a leaf with no editor still does nothing visible" stays — the React side detects equality and chooses no-op. But now the kernel never returns None.

### `SpatialRegistry::drill_out`

Today: `fn drill_out(&self, key: SpatialKey) -> Option<Moniker>`.
After: `fn drill_out(&self, key: SpatialKey) -> Moniker`.

- Scope with `parent_zone` → return parent's moniker (unchanged).
- Layer-root scope (no parent) → return focused's own moniker (was None).
- Torn parent reference → return focused's own moniker (was None).
- Unknown key → same shape decision as `next` / `drill_in`.

**This breaks the existing fall-through in `nav.drillOut` that dispatches `app.dismiss` on a None result** (closing the topmost layer). Updated semantics: `nav.drillOut` compares result to focused. If equal, no zone-level drill happened; dispatch `app.dismiss` to handle the layer-pop case (close inspector, palette, etc.). The user-observable behavior is unchanged; only the internal signal changes.

## Approach

### 1. Kernel — flip the return types

`swissarmyhammer-focus/src/registry.rs` and `swissarmyhammer-focus/src/navigate.rs`:

- `BeamNavStrategy::next` → `Moniker` (or `Result<Moniker, NavError>`).
- `cardinal_cascade`, `edge_command`, `edge_command_from_candidates` → `Moniker`.
- `parent_zone_in_same_layer` stays `Option<&FocusZone>` — internal helper, not part of the public contract.
- `check_override` → translate the wall case (`Some(None)`) to `Some(focused.moniker.clone())` at the cascade level so the change doesn't leak into the override resolver.
- `SpatialRegistry::drill_in`, `SpatialRegistry::drill_out` → `Moniker`.
- `SpatialState` wrappers in `swissarmyhammer-focus/src/state.rs` → match the new shape.

Pick `Result<Moniker, NavError>` over `Moniker` for the unknown-key case if (and only if) the React side benefits from typed error handling. Otherwise have the React side pass the focused `Moniker` explicitly so the kernel can echo it back.

### 2. Tauri commands — propagate the new shape

`kanban-app/src/spatial.rs` (or wherever the Tauri bindings live) — `spatial_navigate`, `spatial_drill_in`, `spatial_drill_out` change return type. Wire shape between Rust and JS becomes `Moniker` (a string brand) instead of `Moniker | null`.

### 3. React side — adjust callers

`kanban-app/ui/src/lib/spatial-focus-context.tsx`:

- `SpatialFocusActions.navigate(key, direction)` returns `Promise<Moniker>` (not `Promise<Moniker | null>`).
- `drillIn(key)` and `drillOut(key)` likewise.

`kanban-app/ui/src/components/app-shell.tsx`:

- `buildNavCommands` (NAV_COMMAND_SPEC closures): no logic change — just `await actions.navigate(key, dir)` then `setFocus(result)`. No null check. If the result equals the previous focused moniker, `setFocus` is idempotent (the entity-focus store is identity-stable on the same moniker), so a "no motion" press is genuinely a no-op without any conditional.
- `nav.drillIn` closure: same — `setFocus(result)`. The "leaf with no editor" case is now a no-op via setFocus(self), not via skipping the dispatch.
- `nav.drillOut` closure: compare result to current focused moniker. If equal, dispatch `app.dismiss`; otherwise `setFocus(result)`. The compare needs the previous focused moniker — read it from the focused-key's entry, or have the closure capture it before the await.

`kanban-app/ui/src/components/fields/field.tsx` (where `01KQ9X3A9NMRYK50GWP4S4ZMJ4` introduces `field.edit`, possibly already merged or not yet):

- Update `field.edit`'s execute closure per the snippet above: drillIn → if result moniker !== focused moniker, setFocus; else onEdit.
- This makes `01KQ9X3A9NMRYK50GWP4S4ZMJ4` and `01KQ9ZJHRXCY8Z5YT6RF4SG6EK`'s drill-into-pills logic both fall out naturally — the same compare-to-focused check handles both "drilled into pill" (different moniker) and "no children, fall through to edit" (same moniker).

### 4. Tests — update every existing assertion

Every test that asserts `assert_eq!(target, None)` or `expect(result).toBeNull()` becomes either an equality check against the focused moniker, or — where it makes sense — a removed assertion (the test was checking a contract that no longer exists). New tests pin the focused-moniker fallback at every previously-None path.

The full list of tests to update:

- `swissarmyhammer-focus/tests/drill.rs` — every `assert_eq!(target, None)` becomes `assert_eq!(target, focused.moniker)`.
- `swissarmyhammer-focus/tests/navigate.rs` — same for cardinal nav at layer-root cases.
- `swissarmyhammer-focus/tests/walls.rs` (or equivalent) — wall override now returns focused.moniker.
- `kanban-app/ui/src/components/*.test.tsx` — any spatial-nav test asserting `null`/`undefined` from a kernel call.

### 5. Document the contract

Top-of-module docstring on `swissarmyhammer-focus/src/lib.rs` (or `navigate.rs`) explaining the contract: nav/drill APIs always return a `Moniker`. "No motion possible" is communicated by returning the focused entry's own moniker. Torn-state surfaces as `Result::Err` (Option A) or echoes the input moniker (Option B). The React side detects "stay put" by comparing the returned moniker to the previous focused moniker.

## Acceptance Criteria

 Replace the kernel's `Option<Moniker>` return shape on `BeamNavStrategy::next`, `SpatialRegistry::drill_in`, and `SpatialRegistry::drill_out` with a non-optional `Moniker`. When motion is not possible, the kernel **returns the focused entry's own moniker** rather than `None`. When the kernel hits a torn-state / unknown-key path, it **emits a `tracing::error!`** with enough context to debug AND echoes back the input moniker so the React side has a valid result. The React side never sees a silent "kernel had no answer" state; every nav / drill dispatch produces an observable focus state, and every actual error is visible in logs.

-This is an architectural change. The current `Option` shape lets every "no motion possible" case look identical at the call site to "kernel lost the registry reference" — torn-state bugs, race conditions during virtualizer remounts, and stale rects all silently produce `None` and the user sees nothing happen. The fix flips the contract so silence is impossible AND torn-state surfaces in tracing.
-
-## Two principles
-
-**1. No motion → return focused moniker.** A semantic "stay put" — wall override, layer-root edge, leaf with no children, drill-out at root. The kernel returns the focused entry's own moniker. The React side does `setFocus(result)`; if it equals the prior focus, that's an idempotent no-op via the entity-focus store's identity-stable handling. Observable: focus stays where it was, no null blip.
-
-**2. Torn state → trace error AND echo input.** A genuine error — unknown SpatialKey, orphan parent reference, registry inconsistency. The kernel emits `tracing::error!(spatial_key = %key, moniker = %input_moniker, ?context, "kernel torn state: …")` and returns the input moniker so the call site has a valid value. The React side's user-observable behavior is identical to the "no motion" case (focus stays put), but ops / devs can see the error in logs and chase it.
-
-This pair eliminates the need for `Option<Moniker>` or `Result<Moniker, NavError>` on the public API. The contract is uniform: every nav / drill API returns `Moniker`. Errors are observability, not control flow.
-
-## Why None is wrong everywhere
-
-Per the enumeration we walked through, the kernel today returns None across twelve distinct paths spanning three APIs. Some are deliberate semantics (override wall, layer-root, leaf has no children) and some are torn state (unknown key, orphan parent reference). The call site cannot tell them apart, and the React side falls through silently in either case. Concrete consequences observed in the project:
-
-- **Click-to-focus failures inside scrolled columns** (`01KQ9XBAG5P9W3JREQYNGAYM8Y`) — partly because `focus(unknownKey)` silently fails when the kernel hasn't yet registered the freshly-minted SpatialKey. After this fix: tracing logs the unknown-key event, observable in dev console, recoverable in user-flow.
-- **Up/Down nav going to "no focus"** (`01KQ5PEHWTEVTKPS2JHSZTXNBE`, `01KQ9XBAG5P9W3JREQYNGAYM8Y`) — partly because stale rects produce no in-beam candidate and the cascade returns None at layer-root. After this fix: nav returns the focused moniker so the user sees focus stay put, not vanish.
-- **Inspector field nav requiring users to click to recover** — the unified cascade *almost* always falls through to drill-out, but the layer-root edge case still returns None. After this fix: focus stays put visibly.
-
-## Scope of the contract change
-
-Three APIs change shape — input becomes `(SpatialKey, Moniker)` (the React side already has both — `useFocusedScope()` reports the moniker, the FocusScope's `keyRef` holds the SpatialKey), output becomes `Moniker`.
-
-### `BeamNavStrategy::next` (cardinal + edge)
-
-Today: `fn next(&self, registry: &SpatialRegistry, focused: &SpatialKey, direction: Direction) -> Option<Moniker>`.
-
-After: `fn next(&self, registry: &SpatialRegistry, focused: &SpatialKey, focused_moniker: &Moniker, direction: Direction) -> Moniker`.
-
-- Override wall (`overrides: { right: None }`) → return focused_moniker (focus stays put visibly).
-- Layer-root with no peers and no parent to escalate to → return focused_moniker.
-- Cross-layer barrier (parent zone is in a different `LayerKey`) → return focused_moniker.
-- Torn parent reference (parent zone unregistered) → `tracing::error!(focused = %focused, parent_zone_key = %orphan, "parent_zone unregistered for focused scope")` then return focused_moniker.
-- Unknown focused SpatialKey → `tracing::error!(focused = %focused, "unknown focused key passed to BeamNavStrategy::next")` then return focused_moniker.
-- Edge commands (`First/Last/RowStart/RowEnd`) — already include focused in the candidate pool, so nothing functionally changes; only the type does.
-
-### `SpatialRegistry::drill_in`
-
-Today: `fn drill_in(&self, key: SpatialKey) -> Option<Moniker>`.
-
-After: `fn drill_in(&self, key: SpatialKey, focused_moniker: &Moniker) -> Moniker`.
-
-- Zone with children → return first/remembered child's moniker (unchanged behavior).
-- Zone with no children → return focused_moniker (was None).
-- Leaf focused → return focused_moniker (was None).
-- Unknown key → `tracing::error!` and return focused_moniker.
-
-**This breaks the existing fall-through pattern in `nav.drillIn` and `field.edit`** that depends on None to mean "fall through to edit / no-op". The new contract: callers compare the returned moniker to the focused moniker. If equal, no descent happened; the caller decides what else to do (edit mode, no-op). If different, focus moves to the returned moniker.
-
-So `field.edit` becomes:
-
-```ts
-const result = await actions.drillIn(fieldZoneKey, fieldMoniker);
-if (result !== fieldMoniker) {
-  setFocus(result);
-  return;
-}
-// drillIn was a no-op (no children) — fall through to edit mode.
-onEdit?.();
-```
-
-### `SpatialRegistry::drill_out`
-
-Today: `fn drill_out(&self, key: SpatialKey) -> Option<Moniker>`.
-
-After: `fn drill_out(&self, key: SpatialKey, focused_moniker: &Moniker) -> Moniker`.
-
-- Scope with `parent_zone` → return parent's moniker (unchanged).
-- Layer-root scope (no parent) → return focused_moniker (was None).
-- Torn parent reference → `tracing::error!` and return focused_moniker.
-- Unknown key → `tracing::error!` and return focused_moniker.
-
-**This breaks the existing fall-through in `nav.drillOut` that dispatches `app.dismiss` on a None result** (closing the topmost layer). Updated semantics: `nav.drillOut` compares result to focused_moniker. If equal, no zone-level drill happened; dispatch `app.dismiss` to handle the layer-pop case (close inspector, palette, etc.). The user-observable behavior is unchanged; only the internal signal changes.
-
-## Approach
-
-### 1. Kernel — flip the return types and add tracing
-
-`swissarmyhammer-focus/src/registry.rs` and `swissarmyhammer-focus/src/navigate.rs`:
-
-- `BeamNavStrategy::next` → `Moniker`. Add `focused_moniker: &Moniker` parameter.
-- `cardinal_cascade`, `edge_command`, `edge_command_from_candidates` → `Moniker`. Thread `focused_moniker` through.
-- `parent_zone_in_same_layer` stays `Option<&FocusZone>` — internal helper. The `None` case at the call site triggers `tracing::error!` for torn-state cases (parent ref points to unregistered key) before falling back to focused_moniker. Layer-root (well-formed `parent_zone = None`) does NOT trace — it's not an error, just an edge.
-- `check_override` → translate the wall case (`Some(None)`) to `Some(focused_moniker.clone())` at the cascade level so the change doesn't leak into the override resolver.
-- `SpatialRegistry::drill_in`, `SpatialRegistry::drill_out` → `Moniker`, take `focused_moniker: &Moniker`. Add `tracing::error!` on unknown-key and torn-parent paths.
-- `SpatialState` wrappers in `swissarmyhammer-focus/src/state.rs` → match the new shape.
-
-Tracing shape — every error log includes:
-- The kernel operation (`nav`, `drill_in`, `drill_out`).
-- The relevant `SpatialKey` (or both, when an orphan parent ref).
-- The `Moniker` being echoed back.
-- A short message identifying the torn-state class.
-
-Example: `tracing::error!(op = "drill_out", focused_key = %key, focused_moniker = %focused_moniker, parent_zone_key = %parent, "parent_zone references unregistered scope");`
-
-Distinguish "well-formed edge" from "torn state": the kernel must NOT trace on layer-root, override walls, leaves, or empty zones — those are normal semantic outcomes, not errors. Tracing fires only on the genuinely-broken paths (unknown SpatialKey, orphan parent ref).
-
-### 2. Tauri commands — propagate the new shape
-
-`kanban-app/src/spatial.rs` (or wherever the Tauri bindings live) — `spatial_navigate`, `spatial_drill_in`, `spatial_drill_out` change signatures: take `focused_moniker` as an additional argument, return `Moniker` (a string brand) instead of `Moniker | null`.
-
-### 3. React side — adjust callers
-
-`kanban-app/ui/src/lib/spatial-focus-context.tsx`:
-
-- `SpatialFocusActions.navigate(key, focusedMoniker, direction)` returns `Promise<Moniker>` (not `Promise<Moniker | null>`). Add `focusedMoniker` to the signature.
-- `drillIn(key, focusedMoniker)` and `drillOut(key, focusedMoniker)` likewise.
-
-`kanban-app/ui/src/components/app-shell.tsx`:
-
-- `buildNavCommands` (NAV_COMMAND_SPEC closures): read the focused moniker from `useFocusedScope()` (or from the spatial actions' state mirror); pass it to `actions.navigate`. Then `setFocus(result)`. No null check, no equality-to-focused short-circuit needed: the entity-focus store's `setFocus` is idempotent on the same moniker.
-- `nav.drillIn` closure: same pattern — pass `focusedMoniker`, then `setFocus(result)`.
-- `nav.drillOut` closure: pass `focusedMoniker`, then compare result to `focusedMoniker`. If equal, dispatch `app.dismiss`; otherwise `setFocus(result)`.
-
-`kanban-app/ui/src/components/fields/field.tsx` (where `01KQ9X3A9NMRYK50GWP4S4ZMJ4`'s `field.edit` lives):
-
-- `field.edit`'s execute: call `drillIn(fieldZoneKey, fieldMoniker)`, compare result to `fieldMoniker`. If equal, `onEdit?.()`. If different, `setFocus(result)`.
-
-### 4. Tests — update every existing assertion
-
-Every test that asserts `assert_eq!(target, None)` or `expect(result).toBeNull()` becomes either an equality check against the focused moniker, or — where it makes sense — a removed assertion (the test was checking a contract that no longer exists). New tests pin the focused-moniker fallback at every previously-None path.
-
-The full list of tests to update:
-
-- `swissarmyhammer-focus/tests/drill.rs` — every `assert_eq!(target, None)` becomes `assert_eq!(target, focused_moniker)`.
-- `swissarmyhammer-focus/tests/navigate.rs` — same for cardinal nav at layer-root cases.
-- Any wall-override test — wall now returns focused_moniker.
-- `kanban-app/ui/src/components/*.test.tsx` — any spatial-nav test asserting `null` from a kernel call.
-
-### 5. Tracing tests — verify the right paths trace
-
-Add tests using `tracing-subscriber::test` or `tracing::collect::with_default` capture to assert that the kernel emits `tracing::error!` exactly on torn-state paths and never on semantic-edge paths. This is the regression guard for "did we accidentally start logging on every well-formed wall override?"
-
-### 6. Document the contract
-
-Top-of-module docstring on `swissarmyhammer-focus/src/lib.rs` (or `navigate.rs`):
-
-> Nav and drill APIs always return a `Moniker`. "No motion possible" is communicated by returning the focused entry's own moniker — the React side detects "stay put" by comparing the returned moniker to the previous focused moniker. Torn state (unknown key, orphan parent reference) emits `tracing::error!` and echoes the input moniker so the call site has a valid result. There is no `Option` or `Result` on these APIs; silence is impossible.
-

-## Workflow
-
-- Use `/tdd` — write the kernel-level `no_silent_none.rs` tests first, watch them fail (current contract returns None), flip the kernel return types and add tracing, watch them pass. Then update the React side to match the new contract; update existing tests; finally pin the browser-level no-silent-dropout assertions.

When motion is not possible, the kernel **returns the focused entry's own moniker** rather than `None`. When the kernel hits a torn-state / unknown-key path, it **emits a `tracing::error!`** with enough context to debug AND echoes back the input moniker so the React side has a valid result. The React side never sees a silent "kernel had no answer" state; every nav / drill dispatch produces an observable focus state, and every actual error is visible in logs.

-## Status: IMPLEMENTED
+This is an architectural change. The current `Option` shape lets every "no motion possible" case look identical at the call site to "kernel lost the registry reference" — torn-state bugs, race conditions during virtualizer remounts, and stale rects all silently produce `None` and the user sees nothing happen. The fix flips the contract so silence is impossible AND torn-state surfaces in tracing.
+
+## Two principles
+
+**1. No motion → return focused moniker.** A semantic "stay put" — wall override, layer-root edge, leaf with no children, drill-out at root. The kernel returns the focused entry's own moniker. The React side does `setFocus(result)`; if it equals the prior focus, that's an idempotent no-op via the entity-focus store's identity-stable handling. Observable: focus stays where it was, no null blip.
+
+**2. Torn state → trace error AND echo input.** A genuine error — unknown SpatialKey, orphan parent reference, registry inconsistency. The kernel emits `tracing::error!(spatial_key = %key, moniker = %input_moniker, ?context, "kernel torn state: …")` and returns the input moniker so the call site has a valid value. The React side's user-observable behavior is identical to the "no motion" case (focus stays put), but ops / devs can see the error in logs and chase it.
+
+This pair eliminates the need for `Option<Moniker>` or `Result<Moniker, NavError>` on the public API. The contract is uniform: every nav / drill API returns `Moniker`. Errors are observability, not control flow.
+
+## Why None is wrong everywhere
+
+Per the enumeration we walked through, the kernel today returns None across twelve distinct paths spanning three APIs. Some are deliberate semantics (override wall, layer-root, leaf has no children) and some are torn state (unknown key, orphan parent reference). The call site cannot tell them apart, and the React side falls through silently in either case. Concrete consequences observed in the project:
+
+- **Click-to-focus failures inside scrolled columns** (`01KQ9XBAG5P9W3JREQYNGAYM8Y`) — partly because `focus(unknownKey)` silently fails when the kernel hasn't yet registered the freshly-minted SpatialKey. After this fix: tracing logs the unknown-key event, observable in dev console, recoverable in user-flow.
+- **Up/Down nav going to "no focus"** (`01KQ5PEHWTEVTKPS2JHSZTXNBE`, `01KQ9XBAG5P9W3JREQYNGAYM8Y`) — partly because stale rects produce no in-beam candidate and the cascade returns None at layer-root. After this fix: nav returns the focused moniker so the user sees focus stay put, not vanish.
+- **Inspector field nav requiring users to click to recover** — the unified cascade *almost* always falls through to drill-out, but the layer-root edge case still returns None. After this fix: focus stays put visibly.
+
+## Scope of the contract change
+
+Three APIs change shape — input becomes `(SpatialKey, Moniker)` (the React side already has both — `useFocusedScope()` reports the moniker, the FocusScope's `keyRef` holds the SpatialKey), output becomes `Moniker`.

+### `SpatialRegistry::drill_in`

+Today: `fn drill_in(&self, key: SpatialKey) -> Option<Moniker>`.

When motion is not possible, the kernel **returns the focused entry's own moniker** rather than `None`. When the kernel hits a torn-state / unknown-key path, it **emits a `tracing::error!`** with enough context to debug AND echoes back the input moniker so the React side has a valid result. The React side never sees a silent "kernel had no answer" state; every nav / drill dispatch produces an observable focus state, and every actual error is visible in logs.

-This is an architectural change. The current `Option` shape lets every "no motion possible" case look identical at the call site to "kernel lost the registry reference" — torn-state bugs, race conditions during virtualizer remounts, and stale rects all silently produce `None` and the user sees nothing happen. The fix flips the contract so silence is impossible AND torn-state surfaces in tracing.
-
-## Two principles
-
-**1. No motion → return focused moniker.** A semantic "stay put" — wall override, layer-root edge, leaf with no children, drill-out at root. The kernel returns the focused entry's own moniker. The React side does `setFocus(result)`; if it equals the prior focus, that's an idempotent no-op via the entity-focus store's identity-stable handling. Observable: focus stays where it was, no null blip.
-
-**2. Torn state → trace error AND echo input.** A genuine error — unknown SpatialKey, orphan parent reference, registry inconsistency. The kernel emits `tracing::error!(spatial_key = %key, moniker = %input_moniker, ?context, "kernel torn state: …")` and returns the input moniker so the call site has a valid value. The React side's user-observable behavior is identical to the "no motion" case (focus stays put), but ops / devs can see the error in logs and chase it.
-
-This pair eliminates the need for `Option<Moniker>` or `Result<Moniker, NavError>` on the public API. The contract is uniform: every nav / drill API returns `Moniker`. Errors are observability, not control flow.
-
-## Why None is wrong everywhere
-
-Per the enumeration we walked through, the kernel today returns None across twelve distinct paths spanning three APIs. Some are deliberate semantics (override wall, layer-root, leaf has no children) and some are torn state (unknown key, orphan parent reference). The call site cannot tell them apart, and the React side falls through silently in either case. Concrete consequences observed in the project:
-
-- **Click-to-focus failures inside scrolled columns** (`01KQ9XBAG5P9W3JREQYNGAYM8Y`) — partly because `focus(unknownKey)` silently fails when the kernel hasn't yet registered the freshly-minted SpatialKey. After this fix: tracing logs the unknown-key event, observable in dev console, recoverable in user-flow.
-- **Up/Down nav going to "no focus"** (`01KQ5PEHWTEVTKPS2JHSZTXNBE`, `01KQ9XBAG5P9W3JREQYNGAYM8Y`) — partly because stale rects produce no in-beam candidate and the cascade returns None at layer-root. After this fix: nav returns the focused moniker so the user sees focus stay put, not vanish.
-- **Inspector field nav requiring users to click to recover** — the unified cascade *almost* always falls through to drill-out, but the layer-root edge case still returns None. After this fix: focus stays put visibly.

 Replace the kernel's `Option<Moniker>` return shape on `BeamNavStrategy::next`, `SpatialRegistry::drill_in`, and `SpatialRegistry::drill_out` with a non-optional `Moniker`. When motion is not possible, the kernel **returns the focused entry's own moniker** rather than `None`. When the kernel hits a torn-state / unknown-key path, it **emits a `tracing::error!`** with enough context to debug AND echoes back the input moniker so the React side has a valid result. The React side never sees a silent "kernel had no answer" state; every nav / drill dispatch produces an observable focus state, and every actual error is visible in logs.

-## Status: IMPLEMENTED
+This is an architectural change. The current `Option` shape lets every "no motion possible" case look identical at the call site to "kernel lost the registry reference" — torn-state bugs, race conditions during virtualizer remounts, and stale rects all silently produce `None` and the user sees nothing happen. The fix flips the contract so silence is impossible AND torn-state surfaces in tracing.
+
+## Two principles
+
+**1. No motion → return focused moniker.** A semantic "stay put" — wall override, layer-root edge, leaf with no children, drill-out at root. The kernel returns the focused entry's own moniker. The React side does `setFocus(result)`; if it equals the prior focus, that's an idempotent no-op via the entity-focus store's identity-stable handling. Observable: focus stays where it was, no null blip.
+
+**2. Torn state → trace error AND echo input.** A genuine error — unknown SpatialKey, orphan parent reference, registry inconsistency. The kernel emits `tracing::error!(spatial_key = %key, moniker = %input_moniker, ?context, "kernel torn state: …")` and returns the input moniker so the call site has a valid value. The React side's user-observable behavior is identical to the "no motion" case (focus stays put), but ops / devs can see the error in logs and chase it.
+
+This pair eliminates the need for `Option<Moniker>` or `Result<Moniker, NavError>` on the public API. The contract is uniform: every nav / drill API returns `Moniker`. Errors are observability, not control flow.
+
+## Why None is wrong everywhere
+
+Per the enumeration we walked through, the kernel today returns None across twelve distinct paths spanning three APIs. Some are deliberate semantics (override wall, layer-root, leaf has no children) and some are torn state (unknown key, orphan parent reference). The call site cannot tell them apart, and the React side falls through silently in either case. Concrete consequences observed in the project:
+
+- **Click-to-focus failures inside scrolled columns** (`01KQ9XBAG5P9W3JREQYNGAYM8Y`) — partly because `focus(unknownKey)` silently fails when the kernel hasn't yet registered the freshly-minted SpatialKey. After this fix: tracing logs the unknown-key event, observable in dev console, recoverable in user-flow.
+- **Up/Down nav going to "no focus"** (`01KQ5PEHWTEVTKPS2JHSZTXNBE`, `01KQ9XBAG5P9W3JREQYNGAYM8Y`) — partly because stale rects produce no in-beam candidate and the cascade returns None at layer-root. After this fix: nav returns the focused moniker so the user sees focus stay put, not vanish.
+- **Inspector field nav requiring users to click to recover** — the unified cascade *almost* always falls through to drill-out, but the layer-root edge case still returns None. After this fix: focus stays put visibly.
+
+## Scope of the contract change
+
+Three APIs change shape — input becomes `(SpatialKey, Moniker)` (the React side already has both — `useFocusedScope()` reports the moniker, the FocusScope's `keyRef` holds the SpatialKey), output becomes `Moniker`.
+
+### `BeamNavStrategy::next` (cardinal + edge)
+
+Today: `fn next(&self, registry: &SpatialRegistry, focused: &SpatialKey, direction: Direction) -> Option<Moniker>`.
+
+After: `fn next(&self, registry: &SpatialRegistry, focused: &SpatialKey, focused_moniker: &Moniker, direction: Direction) -> Moniker`.
+
+- Override wall (`overrides: { right: None }`) → return focused_moniker (focus stays put visibly).
+- Layer-root with no peers and no parent to escalate to → return focused_moniker.
+- Cross-layer barrier (parent zone is in a different `LayerKey`) → return focused_moniker.
+- Torn parent reference (parent zone unregistered) → `tracing::error!(focused = %focused, parent_zone_key = %orphan, "parent_zone unregistered for focused scope")` then return focused_moniker.
+- Unknown focused SpatialKey → `tracing::error!(focused = %focused, "unknown focused key passed to BeamNavStrategy::next")` then return focused_moniker.
+- Edge commands (`First/Last/RowStart/RowEnd`) — already include focused in the candidate pool, so nothing functionally changes; only the type does.
+
+### `SpatialRegistry::drill_in`
+
+Today: `fn drill_in(&self, key: SpatialKey) -> Option<Moniker>`.
+
+After: `fn drill_in(&self, key: SpatialKey, focused_moniker: &Moniker) -> Moniker`.
+
+- Zone with children → return first/remembered child's moniker (unchanged behavior).
+- Zone with no children → return focused_moniker (was None).
+- Leaf focused → return focused_moniker (was None).
+- Unknown key → `tracing::error!` and return focused_moniker.
+
+**This breaks the existing fall-through pattern in `nav.drillIn` and `field.edit`** that depends on None to mean "fall through to edit / no-op". The new contract: callers compare the returned moniker to the focused moniker. If equal, no descent happened; the caller decides what else to do (edit mode, no-op). If different, focus moves to the returned moniker.
+
+So `field.edit` becomes:
+
+```ts
+const result = await actions.drillIn(fieldZoneKey, fieldMoniker);
+if (result !== fieldMoniker) {
+  setFocus(result);
+  return;
+}
+// drillIn was a no-op (no children) — fall through to edit mode.
+onEdit?.();
+```
+
+### `SpatialRegistry::drill_out`
+
+Today: `fn drill_out(&self, key: SpatialKey) -> Option<Moniker>`.
+
+After: `fn drill_out(&self, key: SpatialKey, focused_moniker: &Moniker) -> Moniker`.
+
+- Scope with `parent_zone` → return parent's moniker (unchanged).
+- Layer-root scope (no parent) → return focused_moniker (was None).
+- Torn parent reference → `tracing::error!` and return focused_moniker.
+- Unknown key → `tracing::error!` and return focused_moniker.
+
+**This breaks the existing fall-through in `nav.drillOut` that dispatches `app.dismiss` on a None result** (closing the topmost layer). Updated semantics: `nav.drillOut` compares result to focused_moniker. If equal, no zone-level drill happened; dispatch `app.dismiss` to handle the layer-pop case (close inspector, palette, etc.). The user-observable behavior is unchanged; only the internal signal changes.
+
